2026-04-16T21:16:18.9111468Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-16T21:16:18.9111876Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-16T21:16:18.9112224Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-16T21:16:18.9112517Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-16T21:16:18.9112749Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-16T21:18:39.1187320Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-16T21:17:36.20353894Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":2434890781,\"number\":45223,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":true,\"title\":\"[BUG] Thread starvation in Azure extensions for DataProtection\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\r\\n\\r\\nAzure.Identity.dll 1.12.0+75fb76... 1.1200.24.31701\\r\\n\\r\\n### Describe the bug\\r\\n\\r\\nWe have Azure Web App service on stage environment based on .net 8 and reproducing thread starvation due to `Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection`.  \\r\\nUnfortunately I can't provide a dump, just because I forgot to do it.\\r\\n\\r\\nWe are using\\r\\n* `Microsoft.AspNetCore.DataProtection` (azure keyvault \\u0026 azure blob storage) for auth cookie.\\r\\n* Azure cients with `ExcludeManagedIdentityCredential\\\": false` and all other credentials are excluded for all azure clients\\r\\n\\r\\n### Expected behavior\\r\\n\\r\\nWorks without thread starvation.\\r\\n\\r\\n### My points:\\r\\n\\r\\n1) Looks like it stuck forever in loading keys from azure storage, possible inside in `AzureBlobXmlRepository`.\\r\\n2) Asp.net should detect long requests and give result, at least log error etc. [Link1](https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/30993) [Link2](https://iditect.com/program-example/c--aspnet-core-middleware-that-cancel-a-request-if-taking-to-long.html)\\r\\n3) Looks like `UseRequestTimeouts` does not help here since it will wait result forever also.\\r\\n4) Asp.net should not process new request if dont have resources. [Link1](https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/issues/45277)\\r\\n\\r\\n### Actual behavior\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Reproducing thread starvation due to `Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection`.  \\r\\n2. Requests hang forever\\r\\n\\r\\n## Related links to this problem.\\r\\n\\r\\n* [Creating two instances of DefaultAzureCredential causes requests using the credential to never complete](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/43796)\\r\\n* [Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection.Keys Heavy load causes blocked threads](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/40174)\\r\\n* [8.0.5 Change - Improve usage of Type.GetType when activating types in data protection](https://github.com/dotnet/aspnetcore/pull/54762)\\r\\n\\r\\n### Reproduction Steps\\r\\n\\r\\n## Code for registering data protection services\\r\\n\\r\\n```cs\\r\\n    public static IServiceCollection AddDataProtectionFromConfiguration(\\r\\n        this IServiceCollection serviceDescriptors_, \\r\\n        IConfiguration configuration_)\\r\\n    {\\r\\n        serviceDescriptors_.Configure\\u003cKeyManagementOptions\\u003e(configuration_.GetRequiredSection(DataProtectionConfigurationSections.KEY_MANAGEMENT));\\r\\n\\r\\n        IDataProtectionBuilder dataProtectionBuilder = serviceDescriptors_.AddDataProtection(configuration_.Bind);\\r\\n\\r\\n        AuthenticatedEncryptorConfiguration encryptorConfiguration = new();\\r\\n        configuration_.GetRequiredSection(DataProtectionConfigurationSections.KEY_MANAGEMENT_ALGORITHMS).Bind(encryptorConfiguration);\\r\\n        dataProtectionBuilder.UseCryptographicAlgorithms(encryptorConfiguration);\\r\\n\\r\\n        var keyVaultOptions = configuration_.GetRequiredSection(DataProtectionConfigurationSections.KEY_VAULT).Get\\u003cKeyVaultDataProtectionOptions\\u003e()!;\\r\\n        if (keyVaultOptions.Enabled)\\r\\n        {\\r\\n            var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(keyVaultOptions.Credential);\\r\\n            dataProtectionBuilder.ProtectKeysWithAzureKeyVault(new Uri(keyVaultOptions.KeyIdentifier), credential);\\r\\n        }\\r\\n\\r\\n        var blobsConfiguration = configuration_.GetRequiredSection(DataProtectionConfigurationSections.BLOB_STORAGE);\\r\\n        var blobStorageOptions = blobsConfiguration.Get\\u003cBlobStorageOptions\\u003e()!;\\r\\n        if (blobStorageOptions.Enabled)\\r\\n        {\\r\\n            serviceDescriptors_.AddAzureClients(clientBuilder =\\u003e\\r\\n            {\\r\\n                var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(blobStorageOptions.Credential);\\r\\n                clientBuilder\\r\\n                    .AddBlobServiceClient(blobsConfiguration.GetRequiredSection(DataProtectionConfigurationSections.AZURE_CONNECTION_SUBSECTION))\\r\\n                    .WithCredential(credential)\\r\\n                    ;\\r\\n            });\\r\\n\\r\\n            dataProtectionBuilder.PersistKeysToAzureBlobStorage((serviceProvider) =\\u003e\\r\\n            {\\r\\n                return serviceProvider\\r\\n                    .GetRequiredService\\u003cBlobServiceClient\\u003e()\\r\\n                    .GetBlobContainerClient(blobStorageOpftions.ContainerName)\\r\\n                    .GetBlobClient(blobStorageOptions.BlobName);\\r\\n            });\\r\\n        }\\r\\n\\r\\n        return serviceDescriptors_;\\r\\n    }\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\n## Logs\\r\\n\\r\\nWe can provide more The author requests the SDK dependency be updated to a non-vulnerable version.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Details\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n**Package:** `Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection.Keys 1.6.1`\n**Affected API:** Transitive dependency chain: `Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection.Keys` → `Microsoft.AspNetCore.DataProtection \u003e= 8.0.16` → `System.Security.Cryptography.Xml \u003e= 8.0.2`\n**Scenarios:**\n- Installing `Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection.Keys 1.6.1` pulls in `System.Security.Cryptography.Xml` versions with known high-severity CVEs\n- Users must add a direct package reference to the patched version as a workaround, which is undesirable\n\n**Root ask:** Update the package's transitive dependency on `System.Security.Cryptography.Xml` to a version without security vulnerabilities (GHSA-37gx-xxp4-5rgx, GHSA-w3x6-4m5h-cxqf).\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe issue is a dependency pinning concern — the transitive dependency tree brings in a vulnerable version of `System.Security.Cryptography.Xml`. This is typically resolved by:\n1. Bumping the minimum required version of `Microsoft.AspNetCore.DataProtection` to a version that depends on a patched `System.Security.Cryptography.Xml`\n2. Or adding a direct dependency lower bound on a non-vulnerable `System.Security.Cryptography.Xml` in the package\n\n**Suggested investigation steps:**\n1. Check whether a newer version of `Microsoft.AspNetCore.DataProtection` (above 8.0.16) is available that depends on a fixed `System.Security.Cryptography.Xml`\n2. If so, update the `Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection.Keys` dependency floor and release a new patch version\n3. If the upstream package hasn't been patched yet, consider adding a direct lower-bound reference to a non-vulnerable `System.Security.Cryptography.Xml` version in the `.csproj`\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🏷️ Label Confidence\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Category:** `Client` — This is a client-side SDK package (non-management, non-provisioning), and the issue is about dependency management within the SDK package itself\n- **Service:** `Extensions` — The package `Azure.Extensions.AspNetCore.DataProtection.Keys` lives under `/sdk/extensions/` and the reference issue #45223 (same package area, `customer-reported` + `issue-addressed`) uses the `Extensions` service label\n- **Confidence:** High — The package is explicitly named in the issue, the category is unambiguously Client, and the service label aligns with a quality reference issue\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e👥 Owner Routing\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Extensions` (lines 75–76) — matched on the `Extensions` service label\n- **AzureSdkOwners:** @jsquire\n- **ServiceOwners:** none listed\n- **Routing action:** Assigned `@jsquire`; added `needs-team-attention` label\n- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top; management-specific entries (Mgmt, ARM %Mgmt, etc.) were skipped as this issue has `Client` not `Mgmt`; the `%Extensions` entry at line 75 was the first match with a single AzureSdkOwner\n\u003c/details\u003e","item_number":"58235"},"name":"add_comment"}}}
